script:
if doc.allocated_to:

    employee = frappe.db.get_value(
        "Employee",
        {"user_id": doc.allocated_to},
        ["name", "employee_name", "custom_lab_name"],
        as_dict=True
    )

    if employee:
        # Link field (hidden internally)
        doc.custom_ra_employee = employee.name

        # Visible name field
        doc.custom_ra_employee_name = employee.employee_name

        # Lab
        doc.custom_lab = employee.custom_lab_name

Autofetch rates from GO
script:
# Get the GO name passed from the Client Script
go_name = frappe.form_dict.get("go")
result = []

if go_name:
    # 1. Get all rate history rows linked to this GO
    rate_rows = frappe.get_all(
        "Service Rate History",
        filters={"government_order": go_name},
        fields=["parent", "rate", "effective_from"],
        order_by="effective_from desc"
    )

    seen_services = set()

    for row in rate_rows:
        # 2. Only take the latest rate for each unique service
        if row.parent in seen_services:
            continue
        
        seen_services.add(row.parent)

        # 3. Fetch the Service Item details (Category and Name)
        # Using get_value is faster than get_doc for simple fields
        service_info = frappe.db.get_value(
            "Soil Service Item", 
            row.parent, 
            ["service_name", "service_category"], 
            as_dict=True
        )

        if service_info:
            result.append({
                "service": row.parent,
                "service_name": service_info.service_name,
                "category": service_info.service_category,
                "rate": row.rate,
                "effective_from": row.effective_from
            })

# Return the list to the Client Script
frappe.response["message"] = result
